overview: An Orzhov Standard deck built around gaining life when creatures enter the battlefield to grow key threats and control the board. It offers two upgrade paths: a Bats subtheme focused on flying synergies and scrying, or a pure Lifegain build emphasizing card advantage and aggressive pressure. The strategy aims to delay explosive turns and outlast opponents through steady resource generation.
win_conditions: Growing creatures like Amalia Benavides Aguirre and Essence Channeler through life gain triggers
Going wide with flying creatures and bats to apply pressure
Using Restless Fortress as an auxiliary win condition
Delaying explosive turns to outvalue opponents

keep_criteria: Hands with turn-one plays like Momo, Friendly Flier
Hands containing turn-two threats like Hinterland Sanctifier
Hands with early land drops and life gain triggers
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one early lifegain source or combat damage enabler, and a clear turn-2 play.
Keep any hand with Sheltered by Ghosts or Leonardo, Cutting Edge if supported by a land and a cheap creature or pump spell.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early creatures or lands
Hands heavy on high-cost payoffs without early triggers
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early threats, or 0-1 lands with no lifegain/combat synergies.

examples.reason: Provides a turn-one play, turn-two threat, and early lands matching the deck's low mana curve.

early_game: Play Momo, Friendly Flier on turn one to reduce flying costs and enable early bats
Use Starting Town to pay life for Essence Channeler's ability
Establish early life gain triggers with Case of the Uneaten Feast
Turn 1: Play a Plains, then cast a 1-2 mana lifegain spell or early creature.
Turn 2: Deploy Sheltered by Ghosts or Leonardo, Cutting Edge, then attack with pump spells or blockers to trigger life gain.
Land sequencing: Prioritize Plains for early white mana, then develop into a balanced mana base.
If playing fetch/shock-style lands, sequence them to avoid life loss early when you need it.
mid_game: Accumulate triggers with Hinterland Sanctifier and Zoraline, Cosmos Caller or Voice of Victory
Grow Amalia Benavides Aguirre and Essence Channeler with +1/+1 counters
Use interaction like Erode and Deep-Cavern Bat to clear board and hand
Hoover mana for combat damage triggers and pump spells rather than overcommitting to the board.
use lifegain to offset damage from spells or combat.
If your deck interacts with the graveyard, protect key lifegain sources and avoid unnecessary discards.
Commit creatures when you have active lifegain sources to stabilize life total and trade efficiently.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers, heavy removal, or can answer your key synergies.
late_game: Leverage card advantage engines like Haliya, Guided by Light, Darkstar Augur, and Enduring Innocence
Activate Restless Fortress for additional life gain and pressure
Close out with large, triggered creatures and wide board presence
Leonardo, Cutting Edge + Sheltered by Ghosts + combat damage creates a life gain engine that outpaces most formats.
Win by trading efficiently, pumping creatures, and leveraging life total math to outlast opponents.
Once life is stable (>18), commit to combat damage and pump spells to close the game.
Go all-in when life is stable, you have multiple creatures or combat damage sources, and the opponent has no sweepers or hard removal in hand.
Also commit when you can trigger multiple lifegain sources in a single combat to swing life total decisively.

key_cards.name: Essence Channeler
key_cards.notes: Gains life when creatures enter, synergizes with Starting Town's life loss.

key_cards.name: Amalia Benavides Aguirre
key_cards.notes: Grows with life gain triggers and provides board control.

key_cards.name: Momo, Friendly Flier
key_cards.notes: Reduces flying costs, acts as a bat, and enables turn-one plays.

key_cards.name: Hinterland Sanctifier
key_cards.notes: Accumulates life gain triggers and becomes a 2/3 on turn two with Valley Questcaller.

key_cards.name: Erode
key_cards.notes: Best white removal in Standard, essential for clearing threats.

key_cards.name: Haliya, Guided by Light
key_cards.notes: Generates life gain triggers that guarantee extra draws.

key_cards.name: Restless Fortress
key_cards.notes: Provides life gain that can be offset with Scoured Barrens.

key_cards.name: Darkstar Augur
key_cards.notes: Ensures more resources as the game goes on, synergizes with Momo.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize playing Momo, Friendly Flier on turn one to unlock flying synergies
Use Starting Town's life loss to fuel Essence Channeler early
Offset life loss from Starting Town with Scoured Barrens later
Sequence Hinterland Sanctifier on turn two to maximize early triggers
Common mistakes include overextending without lifegain sources, ignoring life total math, and failing to track opponent's removal or counters.
Unique interactions: Sheltered by Ghosts + pump spells = massive life gain in a single combat.
Leonardo, Cutting Edge synergizes with combat damage and life gain triggers.
Watch for format-specific hate cards like artifact/enchantment removal or sweepers.
Always calculate life total swings before attacking, and prioritize trading into threats over attacking face when life is below 15.

matchups.advice: Use ETB life gain effects to delay their explosive turns and outvalue them in Best of One.

Use multiple cheap threats to force counter windows, stack triggers to out-value counters, and rely on non-creature lifegain or instant-speed pumps. Attack around the stack by committing to board presence and leveraging life gain to survive removal.

Apply early pressure to force life total trades before they stabilize. Use targeted removal on key ramp sources, stabilize with lifegain, and punish overextended boards with combat damage triggers. Don't let them reach their late-game win condition by trading efficiently.

Prioritize lifegain and pump spells to stabilize life total. Trade efficiently, avoid overextending, and use blockers to buy time for your synergies. Once stabilized, shift to combat damage and life gain to out-value their aggressive curve.

Apply consistent pressure with early threats and lifegain to force removal. Use life total math to out-value their removal spells, and win through combat damage once they run out of answers. Don't overcommit to the board if they have sweepers; instead, trade and stabilize.

Win value trades by prioritizing lifegain synergies and combat damage triggers. Use sideboard cards for extra life gain or pump effects, and track life total carefully. Force the opponent to overextend or make bad trades by leveraging your life gain engine.

common_threats: Explosive early-game turns from aggressive decks
Watch for Izzet's cheap removal and stack manipulation, Landfall's ramp triggers, and Prowess's burst turns.
Key tells: Opponent tapping extra mana or holding up counterspells suggests a stack play.
Multiple untapped lands with no immediate play often signals a Landfall or Prowess turn.
Track life total swings to anticipate their win condition.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: runs a mix of effects to gain life when creatures enter the battlefield, creatures that grow with those triggers, and a bat subtheme

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: creatures that grow with those triggers
Restless Fortress as an auxiliary win condition
delay the opponent's most explosive turns

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: playing it on turn one helps with another issue
capable of providing the life loss bonus for Essence Channeler
life gain can be 'offset' with Scoured Barrens
turning it into a 2/3 on turn two

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: too many Rest in Peace and similar cards in sideboards today
